rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER DAMONTE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 25, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND HEATHER FONG'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED MINISTERIAL ACTS IN PARAGRAPHS 14, 15, 16, 17 AND 39. THESE ACTS ARE NOT SUBJECT TO THE IMMUNITIES IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 820.2 (DISCRETIONARY ACTS), 821.6 (INVESTIGATORY PROCESS) AND CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(A) OFFICIAL ACTS PRIVILEGE. TAYLOR V CITY OF LOS ANGELES DEPT. OF WATER AND POWER (2006) 144 CAL APP 4TH 1216, 1239. THE ACTS ALLEGED IN THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PROTECTED BY CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(A). LEAVE TO AMEND IS NOT GRANTED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GWENDOLYN SPEARS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 25, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A RIGHT TO JUDGMENT ON THE ACCOMODATION CLAIM. AS TO THAT CLAIM THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE. IF DEFENDANT HAD KNOWLEDGE, DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO ENGAGE IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION AND DISCRIMINATORY DISCHARGE. DEFENDANT HAS DEMONSTRATED A PERSUASIVE SET OF FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TERMINATED FOR POOR JOB PERFORMANCE. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THE REASONS FOR THIS TERMINATION WAS PRETEXTUAL. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: 2710 BAKER STREET PARTNERS VS. EDWARD SAVIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Edward Savio And Natalie Saylor-Savio'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 25, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT'S EDWARD SAVIO AND NATALIE SAYLOR-SAVIO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE A.L.J. DETERMINED THAT SAVIO AND SAYLOR-SAVIO WERE NOT TENANTS IN POSSESSION AS OF APRIL 2007, THAT DOES NOT MEAN THAT THE RULING APPLIED WHEN PLAINTIFF GAVE NOTICE OF THE RENT INCREASE ON AUGUST 30, 2007. ON THAT DATE, BOTH WERE OCCUPYING THE UNIT WITH THE KNOWLEDGE AND CONSENT OF THE LANDLORD. GIVEN THAT THE ORIGINAL LEASE WAS BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS AND "SAVIO AND OTHERS", SAVIO HAD A RIGHT OF OCCUPANCY IN AUGUST OF 2007. =(302/PJM/PM)

rulings.Case Title: 2710 BAKER STREET PARTNERS VS. EDWARD SAVIO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 25, 2008 LINE 13. PLAINTIFF 2710 BAKER STREET PARTNERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES OF RETALIATORY EVICTION AND DISCRIMINATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF AND DEFENDANT DID NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/PM)
